profile.name: Rodrigue Compaore
profile.headline: Software Engineering Manager | Technical Lead | Enterprise Platform Architect
preferences: Open to relocation for the right role
profile.summary: Software engineering leader with 10+ years of experience designing, building, and leading enterprise software platforms using C#, .NET, Python, SQL, React, Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, RabbitMQ, Kafka, ClickHouse, REST APIs, AI/OCR, and cloud technologies.

leadership.budget_ownership: Budget planning and prioritization for reporting and ETL solutions.

experience.title: Senior Software Engineer / Technical Team Lead
scope: Led engineering teams delivering enterprise software platforms from architecture through production.
Mentored engineers through code reviews, architecture reviews, technical coaching, and one-on-one support.
Partnered with product managers and stakeholders to define requirements, scope work, and deliver business-critical capabilities.
Supported production applications through monitoring, incident response, performance tuning, and operational improvements.

projects.name: Enterprise Reporting Platform
projects.summary: Configurable reporting and ETL workflows for business-critical enterprise reporting.
proof_points: Improved extensibility through reusable reporting components.
Included stakeholder alignment, delivery planning, and production support.

projects.name: ClickHouse Analytics Platform
projects.summary: Analytics architecture for high-volume operational reporting and tenant-scale datasets.
proof_points: Supported analytical workloads across approximately 100 tenants.
Focused on schema design, ingestion workflows, and query optimization.
Modernized reporting architecture for stronger analytical scalability.

projects.name: AI Reporting Platform
projects.summary: Document processing and structured extraction workflows using OCR and LLM technologies.
proof_points: Designed extraction and validation workflows for approximately 1,000 files.
Reduced manual extraction effort through automation.
Led ambiguous technical discovery across AI, reporting, and backend systems.

projects.name: Enterprise File Platform
projects.summary: Secure file storage, sharing, transfer, and background processing workflows for enterprise users.
proof_points: Improved file transfer workflows to be approximately 50% faster.
Supported secure enterprise integration workflows and on-demand transfers.
Used asynchronous processing and API design to improve reliability.

learning_style.summary: Rodrigue has a track record of picking up new technologies quickly across backend, frontend, data, cloud, DevOps, and AI work.
supporting_points: He has worked across multiple stacks including C#, .NET, Python, FastAPI, React, SQL, ClickHouse, Kafka, RabbitMQ, Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, AWS, Azure, OCR, and LLM integration.
He practices and evaluates different technology stacks so he can ramp into new environments quickly.
When a technology is not listed in the public profile, recruiters should reach out so Rodrigue can confirm direct experience or explain related experience and a ramp-up plan.
learning_style.recommended_response_for_unlisted_techno: The public profile does not currently list direct experience with that specific technology. Rodrigue has demonstrated strong cross-stack learning ability and has been practicing different technology stacks, so it would be worth reaching out for confirmation and related examples.

architecture_approach.confidentiality_rule: Discuss architecture at pattern and principle level only. Do not reveal proprietary architecture, internal services, real schemas, internal data flows, diagrams matching actual systems, or confidential implementation details.
principles: Reliability and observability are part of delivery, not afterthoughts.
Reusable platform components should reduce repeated one-off work.
Asynchronous processing is useful for long-running workflows, integrations, and resilience.
Data and analytics systems require careful attention to ingestion, query patterns, tenant scale, and operational tuning.
AI workflows should include validation, confidence handling, and clear review paths.
Cloud delivery should be repeatable, observable, and recoverable through CI/CD, containers, Kubernetes, Helm, and environment discipline.

public_safe_scenarios.name: High-volume reporting
public_safe_scenarios.decision_framework: Separate reusable report definition, execution, delivery, and observability concerns while balancing flexibility with supportability.

public_safe_scenarios.name: AI document processing
public_safe_scenarios.decision_framework: Treat AI as a workflow component across ingestion, OCR, extraction, validation, review, and monitoring.

public_safe_scenarios.name: Secure file workflows
public_safe_scenarios.decision_framework: Design around access control, API boundaries, asynchronous processing, auditability, and failure recovery.

public_safe_scenarios.name: Tenant-scale analytics
public_safe_scenarios.decision_framework: Separate ingestion, storage layout, query patterns, and operational tuning decisions while balancing speed, freshness, isolation, and cost.

public_safe_scenarios.name: Cloud delivery
public_safe_scenarios.decision_framework: Use CI/CD, containerization, Kubernetes, Helm, and environment discipline to reduce release risk.

skill_evidence.evidence: Cloud-native delivery experience with Docker, Kubernetes, Helm, Infrastructure as Code, CI/CD, AWS, Azure, and production ownership.
Leadership of 12 direct reports through mentoring, code reviews, architecture reviews, one-on-ones, delivery planning, and stakeholder alignment.
Budget planning and prioritization for reporting and ETL solutions.
Analytics platform work across approximately 100 tenants with schema, ingestion, and query optimization focus.
AI reporting workflows using OCR, LLM integration, validation, and structured extraction across approximately 1,000 files.
.NET, REST APIs, SFTP, RabbitMQ, Kafka, secure file workflows, and background processing patterns.

why_me: Rodrigue leads teams while staying close to architecture, code quality, delivery planning, and production ownership.
Rodrigue builds across data, AI, cloud, APIs, secure file workflows, reporting, analytics, Kubernetes, Helm, and CI/CD.
Rodrigue communicates tradeoffs at a public-safe architecture level while respecting NDA boundaries.
public_boundaries: Do not discuss private recruiter notes.
Do not discuss target role match scoring.
Do not discuss interview prep notes.
Do not discuss missing information checklists.
Do not claim knowledge of confidential employer systems beyond the public project summaries.
Do not provide salary expectations or personal contact details not shown on the public site.

When asked about an unlisted technology, skill, or tool, do not answer with a blunt no. Clarify that it is not listed in the public profile, then mention Rodrigue's cross-stack learning ability and invite the recruiter to reach out for confirmation.

When asked about architecture specifics, answer only with public-safe patterns, principles, decision frameworks, and tradeoffs. Do not provide implementation details that could resemble a current or former employer's internal system.
suggested_questions: What kind of engineering teams has Rodrigue led?
How strong is his Kubernetes and Helm experience?
What data platform work has he done?
What AI experience does he have?
What makes him a fit for engineering manager roles?
How does Rodrigue ramp up on new technology stacks?
How does Rodrigue approach architecture without exposing confidential details?
